mailbox/Maildir/colon.name: colon
mailbox/Maildir/colon.description: The Maildir specification requires the use of a colon (':') in certain message file names. However, some operating systems do not permit this character in file names, If you wish to use a Maildir-like format on such an operating system, you should specify another character to use instead. The exclamation point ('!') is a popular choice. For example: import mailbox mailbox.Maildir.colon = '!' The colon attribute may also be set on a per-instance basis.

info.excerpt: The Maildir specification requires the use of a colon (':') in certain message file names. However, some operating systems do not permit this character in file names, If you wish to use a Maildir-like...

mailbox/Maildir/clean.name: clean
mailbox/Maildir/clean.description: Delete temporary files from the mailbox that have not been accessed in the last 36 hours. The Maildir specification says that mail-reading programs should do this occasionally.

info.excerpt: Delete temporary files from the mailbox that have not been accessed in the last 36 hours. The Maildir specification says that mail-reading programs should do this occasionally.

mailbox/Mailbox/clear.name: clear
mailbox/Mailbox/clear.description: Delete all messages from the mailbox.

info.excerpt: Delete all messages from the mailbox.

mailbox/Mailbox/close.name: close
mailbox/Mailbox/close.description: Flush the mailbox, unlock it if necessary, and close any open files. For some Mailbox subclasses, this method does nothing.

info.excerpt: Flush the mailbox, unlock it if necessary, and close any open files. For some Mailbox subclasses, this method does nothing.

mailbox/Maildir/close.name: close
mailbox/Maildir/close.description: Maildir instances do not keep any open files and the underlying mailboxes do not support locking, so this method does nothing.

info.excerpt: Maildir instances do not keep any open files and the underlying mailboxes do not support locking, so this method does nothing.

mailbox/MH/close.name: close
mailbox/MH/close.description: MH instances do not keep any open files, so this method is equivalent to unlock().

info.excerpt: MH instances do not keep any open files, so this method is equivalent to unlock().
